Swift divides her writing into three types: "\[quill\](/wiki/Quill "Quill") lyrics", songs rooted in antiquated poeticism; "\[fountain pen\](/wiki/Fountain_pen "Fountain pen") lyrics", based on modern and vivid storylines; and "glitter \[gel pen\](/wiki/Gel_pen "Gel pen") lyrics", which are lively and frivolous.\[\[353\]\](\#cite_note-360) Fans noticed that the fifth track of every Swift album was the most emotionally vulnerable of the album.\[\[354\]\](\#cite_note-361) Awarding her with the Songwriter Icon Award in 2021, the \[National Music Publishers' Association\](/wiki/National_Music_Publishers%27_Association "National Music Publishers' Association") remarked that "no one is more influential when it comes to writing music today".\[\[355\]\](\#cite_note-362) \*\[The Week\](/wiki/The_Week "The Week")\* deemed her the foremost female songwriter of modern times,\[\[356\]\](\#cite_note-363) and the Nashville Songwriters Association International named her Songwriter-Artist of the Decade in 2022.\[\[202\]\](\#cite_note-Dailey-2022-204) Swift has also published two original poems: "Why She Disappeared" and "If You're Anything Like Me".\[\[357\]\](\#cite_note-364)

Critics have analyzed the distinctive qualities and thematic depth in Swift's songwriting. Critic \[Kitty Empire\](/wiki/Kitty_Empire "Kitty Empire") opined in 2024 that Swift is "a profoundly old-fashioned artist", whose songs "tell a story, in succinct, emotive ways that often scan meticulously" unlike most pop music of the time; Empire attributed it to Swift's country beginnings.\[\[358\]\](\#cite_note-365) Swift's \[bridges\](/wiki/Bridge_(music) "Bridge (music)") are often noted as one of the best aspects of her songs,\[\[359\]\](\#cite_note-366)\[\[352\]\](\#cite_note-Siroky-20212-359) earning her the title \["Queen of Bridges"\](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music\#S "Honorific nicknames in popular music") from \*Time\*.\[\[360\]\](\#cite_note-367) \*\[Mojo\](/wiki/Mojo_(magazine) "Mojo (magazine)")\* dubbed her "a sharp narrator with a gift for the \[extended metaphor\](/wiki/Extended_metaphor "Extended metaphor")".\[\[361\]\](\#cite_note-368) Academics have variably described her as a \[poet laureate\](/wiki/Poet_laureate "Poet laureate"),\[\[362\]\](\#cite_note-:72-369) \[philosopher\](/wiki/Philosopher "Philosopher"),\[\[363\]\](\#cite_note-:202-370) and \[bard\](/wiki/Bard "Bard").\[\[294\]\](\#cite_note-Rosen-301)

### Performances

\!\[Swift singing while playing a piano\](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1f/Taylor_Swift_-_Reputation_Tour_Seattle_-_Long_Live-New_Years_Day_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Taylor_Swift_-_Reputation_Tour_Seattle_-_Long_Live-New_Years_Day_%28cropped%29.jpg)

Swift commands large audiences on stage,\[\[364\]\](\#cite_note-371)\[\[365\]\](\#cite_note-:7-372)\[\[366\]\](\#cite_note-McCormick-2023-373) captivating them through emotional storytelling and vocal delivery rather than relying on elaborate dance choreography.\[\[367\]\](\#cite_note-374) According to \[\*V\*\](/wiki/V_(American_magazine) "V (American magazine)") magazine's Greg Krelenstein, she possesses "a rare gift of turning a stadium spectacle into an intimate setting", irrespective of whether she is "plucking a guitar or leading an army of dancers".\[\[368\]\](\#cite_note-VMagazine-375) In a 2008 review of Swift's early performances, \[Sasha Frere-Jones\](/wiki/Sasha_Frere-Jones "Sasha Frere-Jones") of \*The New Yorker\* called Swift a "preternaturally skilled" entertainer with a vibrant stage presence, adding "she returned the crowd's energy with the professionalism she has shown since the age of fourteen."\[\[273\]\](\#cite_note-Prodigy-NY-280) In 2023, Adrian Horton of \*The Guardian\* noted her "seemingly endless stamina" on the Eras Tour,\[\[369\]\](\#cite_note-Horton-2023-376) and \*i\* critic Ilana Kaplan called her showmanship "unparalleled".\[\[370\]\](\#cite_note-377)

Swift's concert productions have been characterized by elaborate \[Broadway\](/wiki/Broadway_theatre "Broadway theatre") theatricality and \[high technology\](/wiki/High_tech "High tech"),\[\[371\]\](\#cite_note-378) and her performances frequently incorporate a live band, with whom she has played and toured since 2007.\[\[372\]\](\#cite_note-379) Swift also often accompanies herself with musical instruments such as \[electric guitar\](/wiki/Electric_guitar "Electric guitar");\[\[373\]\](\#cite_note-380) acoustic guitar; piano;\[\[374\]\](\#cite_note-Burgham_2018-381) and sometimes \[twelve-string guitar\](/wiki/Twelve-string_guitar "Twelve-string guitar"),\[\[375\]\](\#cite_note-382)\[\[376\]\](\#cite_note-383) \[six-string banjo\](/wiki/Banjo_guitar "Banjo guitar"),\[\[377\]\](\#cite_note-384) or \[ukulele\](/wiki/Ukulele "Ukulele").\[\[378\]\](\#cite_note-385) Interacting frequently with the audience, her solo acoustic performances are considered intimate and emotionally resonant, complementing her story-based lyrics and fan connection.\[\[317\]\](\#cite_note-:2-324)\[\[379\]\](\#cite_note-386) Lydia Burgham of \*\[The Spinoff\](/wiki/The_Spinoff "The Spinoff")\* opined that this intimacy remains "integral to her singer-songwriter origins".\[\[380\]\](\#cite_note-387)\[\[374\]\](\#cite_note-Burgham_2018-381) Chris Willman of \*\[Variety\](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")\* called Swift "pop's most approachable superstar",\[\[381\]\](\#cite_note-388) and the 21st century's most popular performer.\[\[382\]\](\#cite_note-Willman-2023-389)

### Video and film

Swift emphasizes visuals as a key creative component of her music-making process.\[\[383\]\](\#cite_note-Ordoña-2022-390) She has collaborated with different directors to produce her music videos, and over time, she has become more involved with writing and directing. She developed the concept and \[treatment\](/wiki/Film_treatment "Film treatment") for "Mean" in 2011\[\[384\]\](\#cite_note-391) and co-directed the music video for "Mine" with \[Roman White\](/wiki/Roman_White "Roman White") the year before.\[\[385\]\](\#cite_note-392) In an interview, White said that Swift "was keenly involved in writing the treatment, casting and wardrobe. And she stayed for both the 15-hour shooting days, even when she wasn't in the scenes."\[\[386\]\](\#cite_note-393)

From 2014 to 2018, Swift collaborated with director \[Joseph Kahn\](/wiki/Joseph_Kahn_(director) "Joseph Kahn (director)") on eight music videos—four each from her albums \*1989\* and \*Reputation\*. Kahn has praised Swift's involvement.\[\[387\]\](\#cite_note-394) She worked with \[American Express\](/wiki/American_Express "American Express") for the "Blank Space" music video (which Kahn directed) and served as an executive producer for the interactive app \*AMEX Unstaged: Taylor Swift Experience\*, for which she won a \[Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Interactive Program\](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Interactive_Program "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Interactive Program") in 2015.\[\[388\]\](\#cite_note-395) Swift produced the music video for "Bad Blood" and won a Grammy Award for Best Music Video in 2016.\[\[389\]\](\#cite_note-396)

Her \[production company\](/wiki/Production_company "Production company"), \[Taylor Swift Productions\](/wiki/Taylor_Swift_Productions "Taylor Swift Productions"), is credited with producing all of her visual media, starting with the 2018 concert documentary \*Reputation Stadium Tour\*.\[\[390\]\](\#cite_note-397) She continued to co-direct music videos for the \*Lover\* singles "Me\!" with \[Dave Meyers\](/wiki/Dave_Meyers_(director) "Dave Meyers (director)"), and "You Need to Calm Down" (also serving as a co-executive producer) and "Lover" with Drew Kirsch,\[\[391\]\](\#cite_note-398) but first ventured into solo direction with the video for "The Man" (which won her the \[MTV Video Music Award for Best Direction\](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Award_for_Best_Direction "MTV Video Music Award for Best Direction")).\[\[392\]\](\#cite_note-399) After \*\[Folklore: The Long Pond Studio Sessions\](/wiki/Folklore:_The_Long_Pond_Studio_Sessions "Folklore: The Long Pond Studio Sessions")\*, Swift debuted as a filmmaker with \*All Too Well: The Short Film\*,\[\[202\]\](\#cite_note-Dailey-2022-204) which made her the first artist to win the Grammy Award for Best Music Video as a solo director.\[\[393\]\](\#cite_note-400) Swift has cited \[Chloé Zhao\](/wiki/Chlo%C3%A9_Zhao "Chloé Zhao"), \[Greta Gerwig\](/wiki/Greta_Gerwig "Greta Gerwig"), \[Nora Ephron\](/wiki/Nora_Ephron "Nora Ephron"), \[Guillermo del Toro\](/wiki/Guillermo_del_Toro "Guillermo del Toro"), \[John Cassavetes\](/wiki/John_Cassavetes "John Cassavetes"), and \[Noah Baumbach\](/wiki/Noah_Baumbach "Noah Baumbach") as filmmaking influences.\[\[383\]\](\#cite_note-Ordoña-2022-390)

## Achievements

\!\[\](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Taylor_Swift_2009_MTV_VMA_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Taylor_Swift_2009_MTV_VMA_%28cropped%29.jpg)

Swift's discography is a "critically hailed songbook", as per \*Time\*'s \[Sam Lansky\](/wiki/Sam_Lansky "Sam Lansky").\[\[394\]\](\#cite_note-time_poty2023-401) She has won 14 \[Grammy Awards\](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") (including four for Album of the Year—the \[most\](/wiki/Grammy_Award_records\#Most_Album_of_the_Year_wins "Grammy Award records") won by an artist),\[\[395\]\](\#cite_note-402) an \[Emmy Award\](/wiki/Emmy_Award "Emmy Award"),\[\[396\]\](\#cite_note-403) 40 American Music Awards (the most won by an artist),\[\[397\]\](\#cite_note-404) 49 \[\*Billboard\* Music Awards\](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award "Billboard Music Award") (the most won by an artist),\[\[398\]\](\#cite_note-405) 118 \*\[Guinness World Records\](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")\*,\[\[399\]\](\#cite_note-406) 30 \[MTV Video Music Awards\](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Awards "MTV Video Music Awards") (including five Video of the Year wins—the most by an act),\[\[400\]\](\#cite_note-407) 12 \[Country Music Association Awards\](/wiki/Country_Music_Association_Awards "Country Music Association Awards") (including the Pinnacle Award),\[\[401\]\](\#cite_note-Lewis-2013-408) eight Academy of Country Music Awards,\[\[402\]\](\#cite_note-409) and two \[Brit Awards\](/wiki/Brit_Awards "Brit Awards").\[\[146\]\](\#cite_note-Jonze-148) As a songwriter, she has been honored by the Nashville Songwriters Association,\[\[58\]\](\#cite_note-GAC-2007-59)\[\[403\]\](\#cite_note-410) the \[Songwriters Hall of Fame\](/wiki/Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame "Songwriters Hall of Fame"), and the National Music Publishers' Association and was the youngest person on \*Rolling Stone\*'s list of the \[100 Greatest Songwriters of All Time\](/wiki/Rolling_Stone%27s_100_Greatest_Songwriters_of_All_Time "Rolling Stone's 100 Greatest Songwriters of All Time") in 2015.\[\[404\]\](\#cite_note-411)\[\[405\]\](\#cite_note-Rolling_Stone_100_Greatest_Songwriters-412) At the 64th \[BMI Awards\](/wiki/BMI_Awards "BMI Awards") in 2016, Swift was the first woman to be honored with an award named after its recipient.\[\[406\]\](\#cite_note-413)

From available data, Swift has amassed over 50 million album sales and 150 million single sales as of 2019,\[\[407\]\](\#cite_note-414)\[\[408\]\](\#cite_note-415)\[\[409\]\](\#cite_note-Lipshutz-2019-416) and 114 million units globally, including 78 billion streams as of 2021.\[\[410\]\](\#cite_note-Charts-2021-417)\[\[411\]\](\#cite_note-BRIT_Awards-2021-418) The \[International Federation of the Phonographic Industry\](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") ranked her as the \[Global Recording Artist of the Year\](/wiki/Global_Recording_Artist_of_the_Year "Global Recording Artist of the Year") for a record five times (2014, 2019, 2022, 2023, and 2024).\[\[412\]\](\#cite_note-419) Swift has the most number-one albums in the UK and Ireland for a female artist in the 21st century,\[\[413\]\](\#cite_note-420)\[\[414\]\](\#cite_note-421) earned the highest income for an artist on Chinese digital music platforms (\[CN¥\](/wiki/Renminbi "Renminbi") 159,000,000 as of 2021),\[\[415\]\](\#cite_note-422) and is the first artist to occupy the entire top five\[\[h\]\](\#cite_note-425) of the \[Australian albums chart\](/wiki/ARIA_Charts "ARIA Charts")\[\[418\]\](\#cite_note-426)\[\[419\]\](\#cite_note-427) and the top ten of the country's singles chart.\[\[420\]\](\#cite_note-428) Swift remains the world's \[highest-grossing touring act ever\](/wiki/List_of_highest-grossing_live_music_artists\#All-time_ranking "List of highest-grossing live music artists"), with cumulative ticket sales at $3.12 billion as of December 2024, per \*\[Pollstar\](/wiki/Pollstar "Pollstar")\*.\[\[421\]\](\#cite_note-429) The Eras Tour is the \[highest-grossing tour of all time\](/wiki/List_of_highest-grossing_concert_tours "List of highest-grossing concert tours") and the first to surpass $1 billion in revenue.\[\[422\]\](\#cite_note-430)\[\[228\]\](\#cite_note-eras_gross-230) Beginning with \*Fearless\*, each of her studio albums have opened with over one million global units.\[\[423\]\](\#cite_note-431)\[\[424\]\](\#cite_note-432) Swift is the most-streamed act on Spotify,\[\[425\]\](\#cite_note-433) and the most-streamed female artist on Apple Music.\[\[426\]\](\#cite_note-434) On Spotify, she is the only female act to amass 100 billion streams,\[\[427\]\](\#cite_note-435) the only artist to have received more than 250 million and 350 million streams in one day (260 million on October 27, 2023, and 380 million on April 19, 2024),\[\[428\]\](\#cite_note-436) and was the first female act to reach 100 million monthly listeners.\[\[429\]\](\#cite_note-437)\[\[197\]\](\#cite_note-time_2023numbers-199) \[The most entries and the most simultaneous entries\](/wiki/Billboard_Global_200\#Most_total_entries "Billboard Global 200") (175 and 34 songs), and most number-ones (5) for a soloist on the \[\*Billboard\* Global 200\](/wiki/Billboard_Global_200 "Billboard Global 200"), are among her feats.\[\[430\]\](\#cite_note-fortnight_glo-438)\[\[431\]\](\#cite_note-439) Swift is the first and only artist to occupy the top nine spots on the Global 200. She has the most entries (154) and top-ten songs (20) on the Global 200 Excl. US. chart.\[\[430\]\](\#cite_note-fortnight_glo-438)

\*Billboard\* ranked Swift at number two on its 2024 "Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century" list\[\[255\]\](\#cite_note-BB-GPS21-258) and number one on its 2025 lists for "Top 100 Women Artists of the 21st Century"\[\[432\]\](\#cite_note-440) as well as "Top Artists of the 21st Century".\[\[433\]\](\#cite_note-441) She has sold over 37.3 million albums as of 2019,\[\[409\]\](\#cite_note-Lipshutz-2019-416) when the magazine placed her eighth on its Greatest of All Time Artists Chart.\[\[434\]\](\#cite_note-442) Twelve of her songs have topped the \*Billboard\* Hot 100.\[\[435\]\](\#cite_note-12n1-443) She is the longest-reigning and the first act to spend at least 100 weeks atop the \[\*Billboard\* Artist 100\](/wiki/Billboard_Artist_100 "Billboard Artist 100") (124 weeks);\[\[436\]\](\#cite_note-444)\[\[437\]\](\#cite_note-445) the soloist with the most cumulative weeks atop and in the top ten of the \*Billboard\* 200 (84 and 425);\[\[438\]\](\#cite_note-ttpd15weeks-446)\[\[439\]\](\#cite_note-447) the woman with the most \*Billboard\* 200 number-ones (14),\[\[440\]\](\#cite_note-ttpd_n1-448) Hot 100 entries (total and single-week: 264 and 32),\[\[441\]\](\#cite_note-449) number-one debuts (7),\[\[i\]\](\#cite_note-451) top-ten songs (59),\[\[435\]\](\#cite_note-12n1-443) top-five songs (36),\[\[197\]\](\#cite_note-time_2023numbers-199) \[Streaming Songs\](/wiki/Streaming_Songs "Streaming Songs") chart-toppers (9),\[\[435\]\](\#cite_note-12n1-443) and weeks atop the \[Top Country Albums\](/wiki/Top_Country_Albums "Top Country Albums") chart (101);\[\[443\]\](\#cite_note-452) and the act with the most number-one songs on \[Pop Airplay\](/wiki/Pop_Airplay "Pop Airplay") (13)\[\[444\]\](\#cite_note-453) and \[Digital Songs\](/wiki/Digital_Songs "Digital Songs") (29).\[\[435\]\](\#cite_note-12n1-443) Swift is the first woman to simultaneously chart five albums in the top 10 and eleven albums on the entire \*Billboard\* 200;\[\[445\]\](\#cite_note-454)\[\[446\]\](\#cite_note-455) and the first act to occupy the top four spots and chart seven albums\[\[j\]\](\#cite_note-457) in the top 10 on the \[Top Album Sales\](/wiki/Top_Album_Sales "Top Album Sales") chart.\[\[448\]\](\#cite_note-458)\[\[449\]\](\#cite_note-459) The \[Recording Industry Association of America\](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA) has certified 137.5 million total units to Swift's name;\[\[450\]\](\#cite_note-460) she is the first woman to have both an album (\*Fearless\*) and a song ("Shake It Off") certified Diamond.\[\[451\]\](\#cite_note-461) Swift is the only artist in Luminate history to have \[seven albums sell over a million copies\](/wiki/Lists_of_fastest-selling_albums\#United_States "Lists of fastest-selling albums") in a week.\[\[452\]\](\#cite_note-Caulfield-2024-462)\[\[453\]\](\#cite_note-463)

Swift has appeared in various power listings. \*Time\* included her on its annual list of the \[100 most influential people\](/wiki/Time_100 "Time 100") in 2010, 2015, and 2019.\[\[454\]\](\#cite_note-464) She was one of the "Silence Breakers" that the magazine spotlighted as \[Person of the Year\](/wiki/Time_Person_of_the_Year "Time Person of the Year") in 2017 for speaking up about sexual assault,\[\[455\]\](\#cite_note-465) and she received the honor again in 2023 for her cultural domination that year.\[\[394\]\](\#cite_note-time_poty2023-401) \*Time\* described Swift as the first Person of the Year to be recognized for "achievement in \[the arts\](/wiki/The_arts "The arts")", as well as the first woman to be recognized and appear on a Person of the Year cover more than once.\[\[456\]\](\#cite_note-466)\[\[394\]\](\#cite_note-time_poty2023-401) She received an \[honorary\](/wiki/Honorary_degree "Honorary degree") \[Doctor of Fine Arts\](/wiki/Doctor_of_Fine_Arts "Doctor of Fine Arts") degree from New York University and served as its \[commencement speaker\](/wiki/Commencement_speaker "Commencement speaker") on May 18, 2022.\[\[202\]\](\#cite_note-Dailey-2022-204)

## Cultural status

\!\[\](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/5.3.10TaylorSwiftByDavidShankbone.jpg/250px-5.3.10TaylorSwiftByDavidShankbone.jpg)

Swift has been credited with making a profound impact on the \[music industry\](/wiki/Music_industry "Music industry"), \[popular culture\](/wiki/Popular_culture "Popular culture"), and the economy.\[\[457\]\](\#cite_note-467)\[\[458\]\](\#cite_note-Khan-2021-468) She dominates cultural conversations,\[\[459\]\](\#cite_note-469)\[\[460\]\](\#cite_note-470) leading some publications to describe her as a cultural "vitality" or \[zeitgeist\](/wiki/Zeitgeist "Zeitgeist").\[\[461\]\](\#cite_note-471)\[\[462\]\](\#cite_note-:1-472)\[\[363\]\](\#cite_note-:202-370) Her music, life, and public image are points of attention in global \[celebrity culture\](/wiki/Celebrity_culture "Celebrity culture").\[\[291\]\](\#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFranssen202290-298) Initially a \[teen idol\](/wiki/Teen_idol "Teen idol"),\[\[463\]\](\#cite_note-473) she has been referred to as a \[pop icon\](/wiki/Pop_icon "Pop icon");\[\[279\]\](\#cite_note-Levine-286)\[\[464\]\](\#cite_note-474) publications describe her enormous popularity and longevity as unwitnessed since the 20th century.\[\[465\]\](\#cite_note-475)\[\[466\]\](\#cite_note-476) In 2013, \*New York\* magazine's Jody Rosen dubbed Swift the "world's biggest pop star" and opined that the trajectory of her stardom has defied established patterns. Rosen added that Swift "falls between \[genres\](/wiki/Genre "Genre"), eras, \[demographics\](/wiki/Demographics "Demographics"), \[paradigms\](/wiki/Paradigm "Paradigm"), trends", leaving her contemporaries "vying for second place".\[\[294\]\](\#cite_note-Rosen-301) Critics regard Swift as a rare yet successful combination of the pop star and singer-songwriter archetypes.\[\[467\]\](\#cite_note-477)

Swift's fans are known as \[Swifties\](/wiki/Swifties "Swifties").\[\[227\]\](\#cite_note-:8-229) \*Billboard\* noted that only a few artists have achieved her level of chart success, critical acclaim, and fan support.\[\[468\]\](\#cite_note-478) Swift's million-selling albums are considered an anomaly in the \[streaming\](/wiki/Music_streaming_service "Music streaming service")-dominated industry following the end of the \[album era\](/wiki/Album_era "Album era") in the 2010s.\[\[469\]\](\#cite_note-bloomberg-479)\[\[470\]\](\#cite_note-nymag10s-480) Economist \[Alan Krueger\](/wiki/Alan_Krueger "Alan Krueger") described Swift as an "economic genius".\[\[471\]\](\#cite_note-481)

Although labeled by the media in her early career as "\[America's Sweetheart\](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")" for her \[girl next door\](/wiki/Girl_next_door "Girl next door") persona,\[\[472\]\](\#cite_note-482)\[\[473\]\](\#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFischerSeidman2016529-483) Swift has been called by detractors "calculated" and manipulative of her image, a narrative bolstered by her 2016 dispute with West.\[\[474\]\](\#cite_note-Vox_VMAs-484)\[\[475\]\](\#cite_note-Vox_2020_call-485) Critics have also noted that her personal life and career have been subject to intense \[misogyny\](/wiki/Misogyny "Misogyny") and "\[slut-shaming\](/wiki/Slut-shaming "Slut-shaming")",\[\[476\]\](\#cite_note-486)\[\[477\]\](\#cite_note-487) as well as rampant media scrutiny and tabloid speculation.\[\[478\]\](\#cite_note-Meddings-2014-488) Swift has also been a victim of numerous \[house break-ins\](/wiki/Burglary "Burglary") and \[stalkers\](/wiki/Stalkers "Stalkers"), some of whom were \[armed\](/wiki/Gun_violence "Gun violence").\[\[479\]\](\#cite_note-489)\[\[480\]\](\#cite_note-490)

Swift's \[private jet\](/wiki/Private_jet "Private jet") use has drawn scrutiny for its \[carbon emissions\](/wiki/Carbon_emissions "Carbon emissions").\[\[481\]\](\#cite_note-491)\[\[482\]\](\#cite_note-492) In 2023, a spokesperson for Swift stated that she had purchased more than double the required \[carbon credits\](/wiki/Carbon_credits "Carbon credits") to offset all tour travel and personal flights.\[\[483\]\](\#cite_note-493)\[\[484\]\](\#cite_note-494) In December 2023, Swift's lawyers sent a \[cease and desist\](/wiki/Cease_and_desist "Cease and desist") letter to American programmer \[Jack Sweeney\](/wiki/Jack_Sweeney "Jack Sweeney") over tracking her private jet, alleging stalking and safety risks. Media outlets have reported that the information posted by Sweeney is a synthesis of publicly available data.\[\[485\]\](\#cite_note-Blistein-495)\[\[486\]\](\#cite_note-496) In February 2024, it was reported that Swift had sold one of her two private jets.\[\[487\]\](\#cite_note-497)

### Legacy

"You have different artists dominating different sectors of the industry: Some are huge at streaming, some are big draws on the road. But we're at this moment where there's no one better than Taylor Swift, whether that's on the radio, with streaming, ticket sales or just \[cultural impact\](/wiki/Cultural_impact_of_Taylor_Swift "Cultural impact of Taylor Swift")."

Swift helped shape the modern country music scene,\[\[489\]\](\#cite_note-guardian_country-499) having extended her success beyond the \[Anglosphere\](/wiki/Anglosphere "Anglosphere"),\[\[294\]\](\#cite_note-Rosen-301)\[\[489\]\](\#cite_note-guardian_country-499) pioneered the use of the internet (\[Myspace\](/wiki/Myspace "Myspace")) as a \[marketing\](/wiki/Marketing "Marketing") tool,\[\[27\]\](\#cite_note-Willman-2007-28)\[\[46\]\](\#cite_note-EW1-47) and introduced the genre to a younger generation.\[\[490\]\](\#cite_note-Roland-2016-500)\[\[294\]\](\#cite_note-Rosen-301) Country labels have since become interested in signing young singers who write their own music;\[\[491\]\](\#cite_note-501) her guitar performances contributed to the "Taylor Swift factor": a phenomenon to which an upsurge in guitar sales to women, a previously ignored demographic, is attributed.\[\[492\]\](\#cite_note-502)\[\[493\]\](\#cite_note-503)

According to \*\[Pitchfork\](/wiki/Pitchfork_(website) "Pitchfork (website)")\*, Swift changed the music landscape with her genre transitions, a discography that accommodates cultural shifts,\[\[494\]\](\#cite_note-504) and \*Billboard\* notes her ability to popularize any sound in mainstream music.\[\[495\]\](\#cite_note-505) Lyrically, in being personal and vulnerable in her songs, music journalist Nick Catucci opined Swift helped make space for later singers like \[Billie Eilish\](/wiki/Billie_Eilish "Billie Eilish"), \[Ariana Grande\](/wiki/Ariana_Grande "Ariana Grande"), and \[Halsey\](/wiki/Halsey_(singer) "Halsey (singer)") to do the same.\[\[173\]\](\#cite_note-Catucci-175) Scholars have highlighted the literary sensibility and \[poptimist\](/wiki/Poptimist "Poptimist") implications of Swift.\[\[326\]\](\#cite_note-Bate-2023-333)\[\[496\]\](\#cite_note-506) She has been credited with legitimizing and popularizing the concept of album "eras".\[\[497\]\](\#cite_note-:132-507)\[\[498\]\](\#cite_note-:11-508) Swift is a subject of academic study and scholarly \[media research\](/wiki/Media_research "Media research").\[\[291\]\](\#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFranssen202290-298) Various educational institutions offer courses on Swift in literary, cultural, and \[sociopolitical\](/wiki/Sociopolitical "Sociopolitical") contexts.\[\[499\]\](\#cite_note-509)\[\[291\]\](\#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFranssen202290-298)

Swift has \[influenced numerous music artists\](/wiki/Cultural_impact_of_Taylor_Swift\#Creative_inspiration "Cultural impact of Taylor Swift"), and her albums have inspired a generation of singer-songwriters.\[\[490\]\](\#cite_note-Roland-2016-500)\[\[285\]\](\#cite_note-Indie_rock-292)\[\[500\]\](\#cite_note-BI_March_2022-510) Journalists praise her ability to reform industry practices, noting how her actions changed streaming policies, prompted awareness of intellectual property in new musicians,\[\[501\]\](\#cite_note-511)\[\[502\]\](\#cite_note-Smith-512) and reshaped ticketing models.\[\[503\]\](\#cite_note-513) Various sources deem Swift's music a paradigm representing the \[millennial generation\](/wiki/Millennial_generation "Millennial generation");\[\[504\]\](\#cite_note-514) \*Vox\* and \*The Daily Telegraph\* likened her to Bruce Springsteen,\[\[505\]\](\#cite_note-VanDerWerff-2020-515)\[\[339\]\](\#cite_note-:0-346) and \*\[The Times\](/wiki/The_Times "The Times")\* named her "the \[Bob Dylan\](/wiki/Bob_Dylan "Bob Dylan") of our age".\[\[506\]\](\#cite_note-516) Swift earned the title \[Woman of the Decade\](/wiki/Billboard_Women_in_Music\#Woman_of_the_Decade_Award "Billboard Women in Music") (2010s) from \*Billboard\*,\[\[507\]\](\#cite_note-517) \[Artist of the Decade\](/wiki/American_Music_Awards\#Artist_of_the_Decade "American Music Awards") (2010s) at the American Music Awards,\[\[508\]\](\#cite_note-518) and \[Global Icon\](/wiki/Brit_Award_for_Outstanding_Contribution_to_Music "Brit Award for Outstanding Contribution to Music") at the Brit Awards for her impact.\[\[411\]\](\#cite_note-BRIT_Awards-2021-418) Senior artists such as \[Madonna\](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna"),\[\[509\]\](\#cite_note-519) \[Stevie Nicks\](/wiki/Stevie_Nicks "Stevie Nicks"),\[\[510\]\](\#cite_note-520) \[Jon Bon Jovi\](/wiki/Jon_Bon_Jovi "Jon Bon Jovi"),\[\[511\]\](\#cite_note-521) Dolly Parton,\[\[512\]\](\#cite_note-522) and \[Elton John\](/wiki/Elton_John "Elton John") have praised her musicianship.\[\[513\]\](\#cite_note-523) \[Carole King\](/wiki/Carole_King "Carole King") regards Swift as her "professional granddaughter" and thanked Swift for "carrying the torch forward".\[\[514\]\](\#cite_note-:3-524) Springsteen called her a "tremendous" writer,\[\[515\]\](\#cite_note-:6-525) while \[Elvis Costello\](/wiki/Elvis_Costello "Elvis Costello") compared Swift's songwriting to that of Bob Dylan,\[\[516\]\](\#cite_note-526) and \[Billy Joel\](/wiki/Billy_Joel "Billy Joel") considered Swift the Beatles' successor.\[\[517\]\](\#cite_note-:5-527)

### Entrepreneurship

Media outlets describe Swift as a savvy businesswoman;\[\[518\]\](\#cite_note-528)\[\[519\]\](\#cite_note-Steele-529) in 2024, she topped \*Billboard\*'s annual Power 100 ranking of the top music industry executives.\[\[520\]\](\#cite_note-530) Swift is known for her traditional album rollouts, consisting of a variety of promotional activities that \*Rolling Stone\* termed as an inescapable "\[multimedia\](/wiki/Multimedia "Multimedia") bonanza".\[\[521\]\](\#cite_note-Curto-2020-531)\[\[522\]\](\#cite_note-532) \[Easter eggs\](/wiki/Easter_egg_(media) "Easter egg (media)") and cryptic teasers became a common practice in contemporary pop music because of Swift.\[\[523\]\](\#cite_note-533) Publications describe her discography as a music "\[universe\](/wiki/Shared_universe "Shared universe")" subject to analysis by fans, critics, and journalists.\[\[524\]\](\#cite_note-534)\[\[525\]\](\#cite_note-EWMay2-535)\[\[526\]\](\#cite_note-Yahr-2022-536) Swift maintains an active presence on social media and a close relationship with fans, to which many journalists attribute her success.\[\[527\]\](\#cite_note-537)\[\[458\]\](\#cite_note-Khan-2021-468)\[\[528\]\](\#cite_note-538) Her in-house \[management\](/wiki/Management "Management") team is called 13 Management.\[\[529\]\](\#cite_note-539)

Swift has endorsed many brands and businesses, having launched clothing lines with \[L.E.I.\](/wiki/L.E.I. "L.E.I.") and \[Stella McCartney\](/wiki/Stella_McCartney "Stella McCartney"),\[\[530\]\](\#cite_note-540)\[\[531\]\](\#cite_note-541) designed \[American Greetings\](/wiki/American_Greetings "American Greetings") cards and \[Jakks Pacific\](/wiki/Jakks_Pacific "Jakks Pacific") dolls,\[\[532\]\](\#cite_note-542)\[\[533\]\](\#cite_note-543) released a number of fragrances with \[Elizabeth Arden\](/wiki/Elizabeth_Arden "Elizabeth Arden"),\[\[534\]\](\#cite_note-544) and signed multi-year deals with \[AT\&T\](/wiki/AT%26T "AT\&amp;T") and \[Capital One\](/wiki/Capital_One "Capital One").\[\[535\]\](\#cite_note-545)\[\[536\]\](\#cite_note-546) She was a spokesperson for the \[National Hockey League\](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League")'s \[Nashville Predators\](/wiki/Nashville_Predators "Nashville Predators") and \[Sony Cyber-shot\](/wiki/Sony_Cyber-shot "Sony Cyber-shot") digital cameras,\[\[537\]\](\#cite_note-547)\[\[538\]\](\#cite_note-548) and became the global ambassador for New York City in 2014 and \[Record Store Day\](/wiki/Record_Store_Day "Record Store Day") in 2022.\[\[539\]\](\#cite_note-549)\[\[540\]\](\#cite_note-Kaufman-2022-550)

### Social activism

Swift identifies as a \[pro-choice\](/wiki/Pro-choice "Pro-choice") \[feminist\](/wiki/Feminist "Feminist"),\[\[541\]\](\#cite_note-GuardianInterview2019-551) and is a founding signatory of the \[Time's Up\](/wiki/Time%27s_Up_(organization) "Time's Up (organization)") movement against sexual harassment.\[\[542\]\](\#cite_note-552) Specifically, she criticized the \[US Supreme Court\](/wiki/US_Supreme_Court "US Supreme Court")'s \[decision to end federal abortion rights\](/wiki/Dobbs_v._Jackson_Women%27s_Health_Organization "Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization") in 2022.\[\[543\]\](\#cite_note-553) Swift also advocates for \[LGBT rights\](/wiki/LGBT_rights "LGBT rights"),\[\[544\]\](\#cite_note-554) and has called for the passing of the \[Equality Act\](/wiki/Equality_Act_(United_States) "Equality Act (United States)"), which prohibits discrimination based on sex, sexual orientation, and gender identity.\[\[545\]\](\#cite_note-555)\[\[546\]\](\#cite_note-Pride2020-556) She performed during \[WorldPride NYC 2019\](/wiki/WorldPride_NYC_2019 "WorldPride NYC 2019") at the \[Stonewall Inn\](/wiki/Stonewall_Inn "Stonewall Inn"), a \[gay rights\](/wiki/Gay_rights "Gay rights") monument, and has donated to the LGBT organizations \[Tennessee Equality Project\](/wiki/Tennessee_Equality_Project "Tennessee Equality Project") and \[GLAAD\](/wiki/GLAAD "GLAAD").\[\[547\]\](\#cite_note-TaylorSwiftLGBTQAlly-557)\[\[548\]\](\#cite_note-558)\[\[549\]\](\#cite_note-559)

A supporter of the \[March for Our Lives\](/wiki/March_for_Our_Lives "March for Our Lives") movement and \[gun control reform\](/wiki/Gun_politics_in_the_United_States "Gun politics in the United States") in the US,\[\[550\]\](\#cite_note-560) Swift is a vocal critic of \[white supremacy\](/wiki/White_supremacy "White supremacy"), racism, and \[police brutality\](/wiki/Police_brutality "Police brutality").\[\[323\]\](\#cite_note-Hiatt-2019-330)\[\[541\]\](\#cite_note-GuardianInterview2019-551) Following the \[George Floyd protests\](/wiki/George_Floyd_protests "George Floyd protests"), she donated to the \[NAACP Legal Defense and Educational Fund\](/wiki/NAACP_Legal_Defense_and_Educational_Fund "NAACP Legal Defense and Educational Fund") and the \[Black Lives Matter\](/wiki/Black_Lives_Matter "Black Lives Matter") movement,\[\[551\]\](\#cite_note-561) called for the \[removal\](/wiki/Removal_of_Confederate_monuments_and_memorials "Removal of Confederate monuments and memorials") of \[Confederate\](/wiki/Confederate_States_of_America "Confederate States of America") monuments in Tennessee,\[\[552\]\](\#cite_note-562) and advocated for \[Juneteenth\](/wiki/Juneteenth "Juneteenth") to become a national holiday.\[\[553\]\](\#cite_note-563) She has openly criticized president \[Donald Trump\](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump").\[\[554\]\](\#cite_note-Schuster_5/19/2023-564) In 2020, Swift urged her fans to check their voter registration ahead of elections, which resulted in 65,000 people registering to vote within one day of her post,\[\[555\]\](\#cite_note-Haag18-565) and endorsed \[Joe Biden\](/wiki/Joe_Biden "Joe Biden") and \[Kamala Harris\](/wiki/Kamala_Harris "Kamala Harris") in the \[2020 US presidential election\](/wiki/2020_US_presidential_election "2020 US presidential election").\[\[556\]\](\#cite_note-566) For the \[2024 election\](/wiki/2024_US_presidential_election "2024 US presidential election"), she endorsed Harris and \[Tim Walz\](/wiki/Tim_Walz "Tim Walz").\[\[557\]\](\#cite_note-567)\[\[558\]\](\#cite_note-568)

## Wealth and philanthropy

Swift became a billionaire in October 2023, becoming the world's first musician to achieve the status "solely based on her songs and performances".\[\[559\]\](\#cite_note-569)\[\[560\]\](\#cite_note-570) As of June 2025, \*\[Forbes\](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes")\* estimates her net worth at $1.6 billion,\[\[561\]\](\#cite_note-Forbes-Profile-571) making her the \[richest female musician in the world\](/wiki/List_of_music_artists_by_net_worth "List of music artists by net worth"),\[\[562\]\](\#cite_note-572) and one of the \[wealthiest celebrities\](/wiki/List_of_celebrities_by_net_worth "List of celebrities by net worth"). \*Forbes\* had previously named her the annual \[top-earning female musician\](/wiki/Forbes_list_of_highest-earning_musicians\#Female "Forbes list of highest-earning musicians") in 2016, 2019, 2021, and 2022.\[\[563\]\](\#cite_note-573) She was the \[highest-paid celebrity\](/wiki/Forbes_Celebrity_100 "Forbes Celebrity 100") of 2016 with $170 million—a feat recognized by \*\[Guinness World Records\](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")\* as the highest annual earnings ever for a female musician,\[\[564\]\](\#cite_note-574) which she herself surpassed with $185 million in 2019.\[\[565\]\](\#cite_note-575) Overall, Swift was listed as the \*Forbes\* \[highest-paid female artist of the 2010s\](/wiki/Forbes_list_of_highest-earning_musicians\#2010s "Forbes list of highest-earning musicians"), earning $825 million.\[\[566\]\](\#cite_note-576) She has also developed a real estate portfolio worth $150 million as of 2023, with residential properties in Nashville, New York City, Los Angeles (\[Samuel Goldwyn Estate\](/wiki/Samuel_Goldwyn_Estate "Samuel Goldwyn Estate")), and \[Rhode Island\](/wiki/Rhode_Island "Rhode Island") (\[High Watch\](/wiki/High_Watch "High Watch")).\[\[567\]\](\#cite_note-577)

Swift ranked first on \[DoSomething\](/wiki/DoSomething "DoSomething")'s 2015 "Gone Good" list,\[\[568\]\](\#cite_note-578) having received the Star of Compassion from the Tennessee Disaster Services and the Big Help Award from the \[Nickelodeon Kids' Choice Awards\](/wiki/Nickelodeon_Kids%27_Choice_Awards "Nickelodeon Kids' Choice Awards") for her "dedication to helping others" and "inspiring others through action".\[\[569\]\](\#cite_note-579)\[\[570\]\](\#cite_note-580) Especially early in her career, Swift donated to various relief funds following natural disasters. In 2009, she donated $100,000 to the Red Cross to help the victims of the \[Iowa flood of 2008\](/wiki/Iowa_flood_of_2008 "Iowa flood of 2008").\[\[571\]\](\#cite_note-581) The same year, she performed at Sydney's \[Sound Relief\](/wiki/Sound_Relief "Sound Relief") concert, which raised money for those impacted by bushfires and flooding.\[\[572\]\](\#cite_note-582) In 2011, Swift used a \[dress rehearsal\](/wiki/Dress_rehearsal "Dress rehearsal") of her Speak Now tour as a benefit concert for victims of \[recent tornadoes\](/wiki/2011_Super_Outbreak "2011 Super Outbreak") in the US, raising more than $750,000.\[\[573\]\](\#cite_note-583) In response to the \[May 2010 Tennessee floods\](/wiki/May_2010_Tennessee_floods "May 2010 Tennessee floods"), she donated $500,000.\[\[574\]\](\#cite_note-584) In 2009, Swift sang at \[BBC\](/wiki/BBC "BBC")'s \[Children in Need\](/wiki/Children_in_Need "Children in Need") concert and raised £13,000 for the cause.\[\[575\]\](\#cite_note-585) In 2016, she donated $1 million to \[Louisiana flood relief\](/wiki/2016_Louisiana_floods "2016 Louisiana floods") efforts and $100,000 to the \[Dolly Parton\](/wiki/Dolly_Parton "Dolly Parton") Fire Fund.\[\[576\]\](\#cite_note-586)\[\[577\]\](\#cite_note-587) Swift donated to \[food banks\](/wiki/Food_bank "Food bank") after \[Hurricane Harvey\](/wiki/Hurricane_Harvey "Hurricane Harvey") struck \[Houston\](/wiki/Houston "Houston") in 2017.\[\[578\]\](\#cite_note-588) Swift donated $1 million for \[Tennessee tornado relief in 2020\](/wiki/2020_Nashville_tornado_outbreak "2020 Nashville tornado outbreak") and \[again in 2023\](/wiki/December_2023_Tennessee_tornado_outbreak "December 2023 Tennessee tornado outbreak"),\[\[579\]\](\#cite_note-589)\[\[580\]\](\#cite_note-590) as well as $5 million toward the reliefs efforts after \[Hurricane Helene\](/wiki/Hurricane_Helene "Hurricane Helene") and \[Hurricane Milton\](/wiki/Hurricane_Milton "Hurricane Milton") in 2024.\[\[581\]\](\#cite_note-591)

Swift has also donated to \[cancer research\](/wiki/Cancer_research "Cancer research"). As recipient of the Academy of Country Music's Entertainer of the Year in 2011, Swift donated $25,000 to \[St. Jude Children's Research Hospital\](/wiki/St._Jude_Children%27s_Research_Hospital "St. Jude Children's Research Hospital"), Tennessee.\[\[582\]\](\#cite_note-592) In 2012, she participated in the \[Stand Up to Cancer\](/wiki/Stand_Up_to_Cancer "Stand Up to Cancer") telethon, performing the charity single "\[Ronan\](/wiki/Ronan_(song) "Ronan (song)")", which she wrote in memory of a four-year-old boy who died of \[neuroblastoma\](/wiki/Neuroblastoma "Neuroblastoma").\[\[583\]\](\#cite_note-593) She has also donated $100,000 to the \[V Foundation for Cancer Research\](/wiki/V_Foundation_for_Cancer_Research "V Foundation for Cancer Research")\[\[584\]\](\#cite_note-594) and $50,000 to the \[Children's Hospital of Philadelphia\](/wiki/Children%27s_Hospital_of_Philadelphia "Children's Hospital of Philadelphia").\[\[585\]\](\#cite_note-595) She has made donations to her fans several times for their medical or academic expenses.\[\[586\]\](\#cite_note-596) During the \[COVID-19 pandemic\](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_the_United_States "COVID-19 pandemic in the United States"), Swift donated to the \[World Health Organization\](/wiki/World_Health_Organization "World Health Organization") and Feeding America\[\[587\]\](\#cite_note-597) and supported independent record stores.\[\[588\]\](\#cite_note-598)\[\[589\]\](\#cite_note-599) Swift performed "\[Soon You'll Get Better\](/wiki/Soon_You%27ll_Get_Better "Soon You'll Get Better")" on the \*\[One World: Together At Home\](/wiki/Together_at_Home\#Television_special "Together at Home")\* television special, a \[benefit concert\](/wiki/Benefit_concert "Benefit concert") curated by \[Lady Gaga\](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga") for \[Global Citizen\](/wiki/Global_Poverty_Project\#Global_Citizen "Global Poverty Project") to raise funds for the World Health Organization's \[COVID-19 Solidarity Response Fund\](/wiki/COVID-19_Solidarity_Response_Fund "COVID-19 Solidarity Response Fund").\[\[590\]\](\#cite_note-600)

She is a supporter of the arts. A benefactor of the \[Nashville Songwriters Hall of Fame\](/wiki/Nashville_Songwriters_Hall_of_Fame "Nashville Songwriters Hall of Fame"),\[\[591\]\](\#cite_note-601) Swift has donated $75,000 to Nashville's \[Hendersonville High School\](/wiki/Hendersonville_High_School_(Tennessee) "Hendersonville High School (Tennessee)") to help refurbish the school auditorium,\[\[592\]\](\#cite_note-602) $4 million to build a new education center at the \[Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum\](/wiki/Country_Music_Hall_of_Fame_and_Museum "Country Music Hall of Fame and Museum") in Nashville,\[\[593\]\](\#cite_note-603) and $100,000 to the \[Nashville Symphony\](/wiki/Nashville_Symphony "Nashville Symphony").\[\[594\]\](\#cite_note-604) In 2012, Swift partnered with Chegg for Good to donate $10,000 to the music departments of six US colleges.\[\[595\]\](\#cite_note-605)

She has also provided one-off donations. In 2007, she partnered with the Tennessee Association of Chiefs of Police to launch a campaign to protect children from online predators.\[\[596\]\](\#cite_note-swift-606) She has donated items to several charities for auction, including the \[UNICEF Tap Project\](/wiki/UNICEF_Tap_Project "UNICEF Tap Project") and \[MusiCares\](/wiki/MusiCares "MusiCares").\[\[597\]\](\#cite_note-607) Swift has also encouraged young people to volunteer in their local communities as part of \[Global Youth Service Day\](/wiki/Global_Youth_Service_Day "Global Youth Service Day").\[\[598\]\](\#cite_note-Hardiman_2007-04-07-608) Also a promoter of children's \[literacy\](/wiki/Literacy "Literacy"), she has donated money and books to schools around the country.\[\[599\]\](\#cite_note-609)\[\[600\]\](\#cite_note-tasteofcountry1-610) In 2018 and 2021, Swift donated to the \[Rape, Abuse & Incest National Network\](/wiki/Rape,_Abuse_%26_Incest_National_Network "Rape, Abuse \&amp; Incest National Network") in honor of Sexual Assault Awareness and Prevention Month.\[\[601\]\](\#cite_note-Billboard-2023-611) Swift donated to fellow singer-songwriter \[Kesha\](/wiki/Kesha "Kesha") to help with her legal battles against \[Dr. Luke\](/wiki/Dr._Luke "Dr. Luke") and to actress \[Mariska Hargitay\](/wiki/Mariska_Hargitay "Mariska Hargitay")'s Joyful Heart Foundation.\[\[601\]\](\#cite_note-Billboard-2023-611)\[\[602\]\](\#cite_note-612)

During the Eras Tour, Swift donated to food banks in Florida, Arizona, and Las Vegas;\[\[603\]\](\#cite_note-613) she also employed local businesses throughout the tour and gave $197 million in \[bonus payments\](/wiki/Bonus_payment "Bonus payment") to her entire crew.\[\[604\]\](\#cite_note-614)\[\[605\]\](\#cite_note-615)\[\[606\]\](\#cite_note-616) In February 2024, she donated $100,000 to the family of a woman who died in a \[shooting at the Kansas City Chiefs' Super Bowl parade\](/wiki/2024_Kansas_City_parade_shooting "2024 Kansas City parade shooting").\[\[607\]\](\#cite_note-617)\[\[608\]\](\#cite_note-618) In December 2024, a week before Christmas, Swift donated $250,000 to Operation Breakthrough. The funds were directed to workforce development, childcare, and early learning programs.\[\[609\]\](\#cite_note-619)

| 2000s | "\[Come On Over\](/wiki/Come_On_Over_(Shania_Twain_song) "Come On Over (Shania Twain song)")" – \[Robert John "Mutt" Lange\](/wiki/Mutt_Lange "Mutt Lange") & \[Shania Twain\](/wiki/Shania_Twain "Shania Twain") (2000) "\[I Hope You Dance\](/wiki/I_Hope_You_Dance "I Hope You Dance")" – \[Mark D. Sanders\](/wiki/Mark_D._Sanders "Mark D. Sanders") & \[Tia Sillers\](/wiki/Tia_Sillers "Tia Sillers") (2001) "\[The Lucky One\](/wiki/The_Lucky_One_(Alison_Krauss_song) "The Lucky One (Alison Krauss song)")" – \[Robert Lee Castleman\](/wiki/Robert_Lee_Castleman "Robert Lee Castleman") (2002) "\[Where Were You (When the World Stopped Turning)\](/wiki/Where_Were_You_(When_the_World_Stopped_Turning) "Where Were You (When the World Stopped Turning)")" – \[Alan Jackson\](/wiki/Alan_Jackson "Alan Jackson") (2003) "\[It's Five O'Clock Somewhere\](/wiki/It%27s_Five_O%27Clock_Somewhere "It's Five O'Clock Somewhere")" – Jim "Moose" Brown & Don Rollins (2004) "\[Live Like You Were Dying\](/wiki/Live_Like_You_Were_Dying_(song) "Live Like You Were Dying (song)")" – \[Tim Nichols\](/wiki/Tim_Nichols "Tim Nichols") & \[Craig Wiseman\](/wiki/Craig_Wiseman "Craig Wiseman") (2005) "\[Bless the Broken Road\](/wiki/Bless_the_Broken_Road "Bless the Broken Road")" – Bobby Boyd, \[Jeff Hanna\](/wiki/Jeff_Hanna "Jeff Hanna"), & \[Marcus Hummon\](/wiki/Marcus_Hummon "Marcus Hummon") (2006) "\[Jesus, Take the Wheel\](/wiki/Jesus,_Take_the_Wheel "Jesus, Take the Wheel")" – \[Hillary Lindsey\](/wiki/Hillary_Lindsey "Hillary Lindsey"), \[Brett James\](/wiki/Brett_James "Brett James") & \[Gordie Sampson\](/wiki/Gordie_Sampson "Gordie Sampson") (2007) "\[Before He Cheats\](/wiki/Before_He_Cheats "Before He Cheats")" – \[Chris Tompkins\](/wiki/Chris_Tompkins "Chris Tompkins") & \[Josh Kear\](/wiki/Josh_Kear "Josh Kear") (2008) "\[Stay\](/wiki/Stay_(Sugarland_song) "Stay (Sugarland song)")" – \[Jennifer Nettles\](/wiki/Jennifer_Nettles "Jennifer Nettles") (2009) |
| 2010s | "\[White Horse\](/wiki/White_Horse_(Taylor_Swift_song) "White Horse (Taylor Swift song)")" – \[Liz Rose\](/wiki/Liz_Rose "Liz Rose") & Taylor Swift (2010) "\[Need You Kelley\](/wiki/Charles_Kelley "Charles Kelley") & \[Hillary Scott\](/wiki/Hillary_Scott "Hillary Scott") (2011) "\[Mean\](/wiki/Mean_(song) "Mean (song)")" – Taylor Swift (2012) "\[Blown Away\](/wiki/Blown_Away_(Carrie_Underwood_song) "Blown Away (Carrie Underwood song)")" – \[Josh Kear\](/wiki/Josh_Kear "Josh Kear") & \[Chris Tompkins\](/wiki/Chris_Tompkins "Chris Tompkins") (2013) – \[Glen Campbell\](/wiki/Glen_Campbell "Glen Campbell") & \[Julian Raymond\](/wiki/Julian_Raymond "Julian Raymond") (2015) "\[Girl Crush\](/wiki/Girl_Crush "Girl Crush")" – \[Hillary | 2000s | "\[Beautiful Stranger\](/wiki/Beautiful_Stranger "Beautiful Stranger")" – \[Madonna\](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna") & \[William Orbit\](/wiki/William_Orbit "William Orbit") (songwriters) (2000) "\[When She Loved Me\](/wiki/When_She_Loved_Me "When She Loved Me")" – \[Randy Newman\](/wiki/Randy_Newman "Randy Newman") (songwriter) (2001) "\[Boss of Me\](/wiki/Boss_of_Me "Boss of Me")" – \[John Flansburgh\](/wiki/John_Flansburgh "John Flansburgh") & \[John Linnell\](/wiki/John_Linnell "John Linnell") (songwriters) (2002) "\[If I Didn't Have You\](/wiki/If_I_Didn%27t_Have_You_(Disney_song) "If I Didn't Have You (Disney song)")" – \[Randy Newman\](/wiki/Randy_Newman "Randy Newman") (songwriter) (2003) "A Mighty Wind" – \[Christopher Guest\](/wiki/Christopher_Guest "Christopher Guest"), \[Eugene Levy\](/wiki/Eugene_Levy "Eugene Levy") & \[Michael McKean\](/wiki/Michael_McKean "Michael McKean") (songwriters) (2004) "\[Into the West\](/wiki/Into_the_West_(song) "Into the West (song)")" – \[Annie Lennox\](/wiki/Annie_Lennox "Annie Lennox"), \[Howard Shore\](/wiki/Howard_Shore "Howard Shore") & \[Fran Walsh\](/wiki/Fran_Walsh "Fran Walsh") (songwriters) (2005) "\[Believe\](/wiki/Believe_(Josh_Groban_song) "Believe (Josh Groban song)")" – \[Glen Ballard\](/wiki/Glen_Ballard "Glen Ballard") & \[Alan Silvestri\](/wiki/Alan_Silvestri "Alan Silvestri") (songwriters) (2006) "\[Our Town\](/wiki/Our_Town_(James_Taylor_song) "Our Town (James Taylor song)")" – \[Randy Newman\](/wiki/Randy_Newman "Randy Newman") (songwriter) (2007) "\[Love You I Do\](/wiki/Love_You_I_Do "Love You I Do")" – \[Siedah Garrett\](/wiki/Siedah_Garrett "Siedah Garrett") & \[Henry Krieger\](/wiki/Henry_Krieger "Henry Krieger") (songwriters) (2008) "\[Down to Earth\](/wiki/Down_to_Earth_(Peter_Gabriel_song) "Down to Earth (Peter Gabriel song)")" – \[Peter Gabriel\](/wiki/Peter_Gabriel "Peter Gabriel") & \[Thomas Newman\](/wiki/Thomas_Newman "Thomas Newman") (songwriters) (2009) |
| 2010s | "\[Jai Ho\](/wiki/Jai_Ho_(song) "Jai Ho (song)")" – \[Gulzar\](/wiki/Gulzar "Gulzar"), \[A. R. Rahman\](/wiki/A._R._Rahman "A. R. "Can't Stop the Feeling\!")" – \[Max Martin\](/wiki/Max_Martin "Max Martin"), \[Shellback\](/wiki/Shellback_(record_producer) "Shellback (record producer)") & \[Justin Timberlake\](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake") (songwriters) (2017) "\[How Far I'll Go\](/wiki/How_Far_I%27ll_Go "How Far I'll Go")" – \[Lin-Manuel Miranda\](/wiki/Lin-Manuel_Miranda "Lin-Manuel Miranda") (songwriter) (2018) "\[Shallow\](/wiki/Shallow_(Lady_Gaga_and_Bradley_Cooper_song) "Shallow (Lady Gaga and Bradley Cooper song)")" – \[Lady Gaga\](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga"), \[Mark Ronson\](/wiki/Mark_Ronson "Mark Ronson"), \[Anthony Rossomando\](/wiki/Anthony_Rossomando "Anthony Rossomando") & \[Andrew Wyatt\](/wiki/Andrew_Wyatt "Andrew Wyatt") (songwriters) (2019) |

"Hey Ya\!")" – \[Outkast\](/wiki/Outkast "Outkast") (2004) "\[Boulevard of Broken Dreams\](/wiki/Boulevard_of_Broken_Dreams_(Green_Day_song) "Boulevard of Broken Dreams (Green Day song)")" – \[Green Day\](/wiki/Green_Day "Green Day") (2005) "\[I Write Sins Not Tragedies\](/wiki/I_Write_Sins_Not_Tragedies "I Write Sins Not Tragedies")" – \[Panic\! at the Disco\](/wiki/Panic\!_at_the_Disco "Panic\! at the Disco") (2006) "\[Umbrella\](/wiki/Umbrella_(song) "Umbrella (song)")" – \[Rihanna\](/wiki/Rihanna "Rihanna") featuring \[Jay-Z\](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") (2007) "\[Piece of Me\](/wiki/Piece_of_Me "Piece of Me")" – \[Britney Spears\](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears") (2008) "\[Single Ladies (Put a Ring on It)\](/wiki/Single_Ladies_(Put_a_Ring_on_It) "Single Ladies (Put a Ring on It)")" – \[Beyoncé\](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé") (2009) |

Now\](/wiki/Need_You_Now_(Lady_Antebellum_album) "Need You Now (Lady Antebellum album)")\* – \[Lady Antebellum\](/wiki/Lady_A "Lady A") (2011) \*\[Own the Night\](/wiki/Own_the_Night "Own the Night")\* – \[Lady Antebellum\](/wiki/Lady_A "Lady A") (2012) \*\[Uncaged\](/wiki/Uncaged "Uncaged")\* – \[Zac Brown Band\](/wiki/Zac_Brown_Band "Zac Brown Band") (2013) \*\[Same Trailer Different Park\](/wiki/Same_Trailer_Different_Park "Same Trailer Different Park")\* – \[Kacey Musgraves\](/wiki/Kacey_Musgraves "Kacey Musgraves") (2014) \*\[Platinum\](/wiki/Platinum_(Miranda_Lambert_album) "Platinum (Miranda Lambert album)")\* – \[Miranda Lambert\](/wiki/Miranda_Lambert "Miranda Lambert") (2015) \*\[Traveller\](/wiki/Traveller_(Chris_Stapleton_album) "Traveller (Chris Stapleton album)")\* – \[Chris Stapleton\](/wiki/Chris_Stapleton "Chris Stapleton") (2016) \*\[A Sailor's Guide to Earth\](/wiki/A_Sailor%27s_Guide_to_Earth "A Sailor's Guide to Earth")\* – \[Sturgill Simpson\](/wiki/Sturgill_Simpson "Sturgill Simpson") (2017) \*\[From A Room: Volume 1\](/wiki/From_A_Room:_Volume_1 "From A Room: Volume 1")\* – \[Chris Stapleton\](/wiki/Chris_Stapleton "Chris Stapleton") (2018) \*\[Golden Hour\](/wiki/Golden_Hour_(Kacey_Musgraves_album) "Golden Hour (Kacey Musgraves album)")\* – \[Kacey Musgraves\](/wiki/Kacey_Musgraves "Kacey Musgraves") (2019) \*\[While I'm Livin'\](/wiki/While_I%27m_Livin%27 "While I'm Livin'")\* – \[Tanya Tucker\](/wiki/Tanya_Tucker "Tanya Tucker") (2020) \*\[Wildcard\](/wiki/Wildcard_(Miranda_Lambert_album) "Wildcard (Miranda \[Chris Stapleton\](/wiki/Chris_Stapleton "Chris Stapleton") (2022) \*\[A Beautiful Time\](/wiki/A_Beautiful_Time "A Beautiful Time")\* – \[Willie Nelson\](/wiki/Willie_Nelson "Willie Nelson") (2023) (1992) "\[Digging in the Dirt\](/wiki/Digging_in_the_Dirt "Digging in the Dirt")" – \[Peter Gabriel\](/wiki/Peter_Gabriel "Peter Gabriel") (1993) "\[Steam\](/wiki/Steam_(Peter_Gabriel_song) "Steam (Peter Gabriel song)")" – \[Peter Gabriel\](/wiki/Peter_Gabriel "Peter Gabriel") (1994) "\[Love Is Strong\](/wiki/Love_Is_Strong "Love Is Strong")" – \[The Rolling Stones\](/wiki/The_Rolling_Stones "The Rolling Stones") (1995) "\[Scream\](/wiki/Scream_(Michael_Jackson_and_Janet_Jackson_song) "Scream (Michael Jackson and Janet Jackson song)")" – \[Michael Jackson\](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") & \[Janet Jackson\](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson") (1996) "\[Free as a Bird\](/wiki/Free_as_a_Bird "Free as a Bird")" – \[The Beatles\](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") (1997) "\[Got 'til It's Gone\](/wiki/Got_%27til_It%27s_Gone "Got 'til It's Gone")" – \[Janet Jackson\](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson") (1998) "\[Ray of Light\](/wiki/Ray_of_Light_(song) "Ray of Light (song)")" – \[Madonna\](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna") (1999) |

| 2010s | "\[Boom Boom Pow\](/wiki/Boom_Boom_Pow "Boom Boom Pow")" – \[Black Eyed Peas\](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Peas "Black Eyed Peas") (2010) "\[Bad Romance\](/wiki/Bad_Romance "Bad Romance")" – \[Lady Gaga\](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga") (2011) "\[Rolling in the Deep\](/wiki/Rolling_in_the_Deep "Rolling in the Deep")" – \[Adele\](/wiki/Adele "Adele") (2012) "\[We Found Love\](/wiki/We_Found_Love_(music_video) "We Found Love (music video)")" – \[Rihanna\](/wiki/Rihanna "Rihanna") featuring \[Calvin Harris\](/wiki/Calvin_Harris "Calvin Harris") (2013) "\[Suit & Tie\](/wiki/Suit_%26_Tie "Suit \&amp; Tie")" – \[Justin Timberlake\](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake") featuring \[Jay-Z\](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") (2014) "\[Happy\](/wiki/Happy_(Pharrell_Williams_song) "Happy (Pharrell Williams song)")" – \[Pharrell Williams\](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams") (2015) "\[Bad Blood\](/wiki/Bad_Blood_(Taylor_Swift_song) "Bad Blood (Taylor Swift song)")" – Taylor Swift featuring \[Kendrick Lamar\](/wiki/Kendrick_Lamar "Kendrick Lamar") (2016) "\[Formation\](/wiki/Formation_(song) "Formation (song)")" – \[Beyoncé\](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé") (2017) "\[Humble\](/wiki/Humble_(song) "Humble (song)")" – \[Kendrick Lamar\](/wiki/Kendrick_Lamar "Kendrick Lamar") (2018) "\[This Is America\](/wiki/This_Is_America_(song) "This Is America (song)")" – \[Childish Gambino\](/wiki/Donald_Glover "Donald Glover") (2019) |
| 2020s | "\[Old Town Road\](/wiki/Old_Town_Road "Old Town Road")" – \[Lil Nas X\](/wiki/Lil_Nas_X "Lil Nas X") featuring \[Billy Ray Cyrus\](/wiki/Billy_Ray_Cyrus "Billy Ray Cyrus") (2020) "\[Brown Skin Girl\](/wiki/Brown_Skin_Girl "Brown Skin Girl")" – \[Beyoncé\](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé"), \[Blue Ivy\](/wiki/Blue_Ivy_Carter "Blue Ivy Carter") & \[Wizkid\](/wiki/Wizkid "Wizkid") (2021) "Freedom" – \[Jon Batiste\](/wiki/Jon_Batiste "Jon Batiste") (2022) \*\[All Too Well: The Short Film\](/wiki/All_Too_Well:_The_Short_Film "All Too Well: The Short Film")\* – Taylor Swift (2023) "\[I'm Only Sleeping\](/wiki/I%27m_Only_Sleeping "I'm Only Sleeping")" – \[The Beatles\](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") (2024) "\[Not Like Us\](/wiki/Not_Like_Us "Not Like Us")" – \[Kendrick Lamar\](/wiki/Kendrick_Lamar "Kendrick Lamar") (2025) |

| 2010s | \*\[The E.N.D.\](/wiki/The_E.N.D. "The E.N.D.")\* – \[Black Eyed Peas\](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Peas "Black Eyed Peas") (2010) \*\[The Fame Monster\](/wiki/The_Fame_Monster "The Fame Monster")\* – \[Lady Gaga\](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga") (2011) \*\[21\](/wiki/21_(Adele_album) "21 (Adele album)")\* – \[Adele\](/wiki/Adele "Adele") (2012) \*\[Stronger\](/wiki/Stronger_(Kelly_Clarkson_album) "Stronger (Kelly Clarkson album)")\* – \[Kelly Clarkson\](/wiki/Kelly_Clarkson "Kelly Clarkson") (2013) \*\[Unorthodox Jukebox\](/wiki/Unorthodox_Jukebox "Unorthodox Jukebox")\* – \[Bruno Mars\](/wiki/Bruno_Mars "Bruno Mars") (2014) \*\[In the Lonely Hour\](/wiki/In_the_Lonely_Hour "In the Lonely Hour")\* – \[Sam Smith\](/wiki/Sam_Smith "Sam Smith") (2015) \*\[1989\](/wiki/1989_(album) "1989 (album)")\* – Taylor Swift (2016) \*\[25\](/wiki/25_(Adele_album) "25 (Adele album)")\* – \[Adele\](/wiki/Adele "Adele") (2017) \*\[÷\](/wiki/%C3%B7_(album) "÷ (album)")\* – \[Ed Sheeran\](/wiki/Ed_Sheeran "Ed Sheeran") (2018) \*\[Sweetener\](/wiki/Sweetener_(album) "Sweetener (album)")\* – \[Ariana Grande\](/wiki/Ariana_Grande "Ariana Grande") (2018) |

Kelly\](/wiki/R._Kelly "R. Kelly") (songwriter) (1998) "\[My Heart Will Go On\](/wiki/My_Heart_Will_Go_On "My Heart Will Go On")" – \[James Horner\](/wiki/James_Horner "James Horner") & \[Will Jennings\](/wiki/Will_Jennings "Will Jennings") (songwriters) (1999) |
| 2000s | "\[Beautiful Stranger\](/wiki/Beautiful_Stranger "Beautiful Stranger")" – \[Madonna\](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna") & \[William Orbit\](/wiki/William_Orbit "William Orbit") (songwriters) (2000) "\[When She Loved Me\](/wiki/When_She_Loved_Me "When She Loved Me")" – \[Randy Newman\](/wiki/Randy_Newman "Randy Newman") (songwriter) (2001) "\[Boss of Me\](/wiki/Boss_of_Me "Boss of Me")" – \[John Flansburgh\](/wiki/John_Flansburgh "John Flansburgh") & \[John Linnell\](/wiki/John_Linnell "John Linnell") (songwriters) (2002) "\[If I Didn't Have You\](/wiki/If_I_Didn%27t_Have_You_(Disney_song) "If I Didn't Have You (Disney song)")" – \[Randy Newman\](/wiki/Randy_Newman "Randy Newman") (songwriter) (2003) "A Mighty Wind" – \[Christopher Guest\](/wiki/Christopher_Guest "Christopher Guest"), \[Eugene Levy\](/wiki/Eugene_Levy "Eugene Levy") & \[Michael McKean\](/wiki/Michael_McKean "Michael McKean") (songwriters) (2004) "\[Into the West\](/wiki/Into_the_West_(song) "Into the West (song)")" – \[Annie Lennox\](/wiki/Annie_Lennox "Annie Lennox"), \[Howard Shore\](/wiki/Howard_Shore "Howard Shore") & \[Fran Walsh\](/wiki/Fran_Walsh "Fran Walsh") (songwriters) (2005) "\[Believe\](/wiki/Believe_(Josh_Groban_song) "Believe (Josh Groban song)")" – \[Glen Ballard\](/wiki/Glen_Ballard "Glen Ballard") & \[Alan Silvestri\](/wiki/Alan_Silvestri "Alan Silvestri") (songwriters) (2006) "\[Our Town\](/wiki/Our_Town_(James_Taylor_song) "Our Town (James Taylor song)")" – \[Randy Newman\](/wiki/Randy_Newman "Randy Newman") (songwriter) (2007) "\[Love You I Do\](/wiki/Love_You_I_Do "Love You I Do")" – \[Siedah Garrett\](/wiki/Siedah_Garrett "Siedah Garrett") & \[Henry Krieger\](/wiki/Henry_Krieger "Henry Krieger") (songwriters) (2008) "\[Down to Earth\](/wiki/Down_to_Earth_(Peter_Gabriel_song) "Down to Earth (Peter Gabriel song)")" – \[Peter Gabriel\](/wiki/Peter_Gabriel "Peter Gabriel") & \[Thomas Newman\](/wiki/Thomas_Newman "Thomas Newman") (songwriters) (2009) |
| 2010s | "\[Jai Ho\](/wiki/Jai_Ho_(song) "Jai Ho (song)")" – \[Gulzar\](/wiki/Gulzar "Gulzar"), \[A. R. Rahman\](/wiki/A._R._Rahman "A. R. Rahman") & \[Tanvi Shah\](/wiki/Tanvi_Shah "Tanvi Shah") (songwriters) (2010) "\[The Weary Kind\](/wiki/The_Weary_Kind "The Weary Kind")" – \[Ryan Bingham\](/wiki/Ryan_Bingham "Ryan Bingham") & \[T Bone Burnett\](/wiki/T_Bone_Burnett "T Bone Burnett") (songwriters) (2011) "\[I See the Light\](/wiki/I_See_the_Light "I See the Light")" – \[Alan Menken\](/wiki/Alan_Menken "Alan Menken") & \[Glenn Slater\](/wiki/Glenn_Slater "Glenn Slater") (songwriters) (2012) "\[Safe & Sound\](/wiki/Safe_%26_Sound_(Taylor_Swift_song) "Safe \&amp; Sound (Taylor Swift song)")" – \[T Bone Burnett\](/wiki/T_Bone_Burnett "T Bone Burnett"), Taylor Swift, \[Joy Williams\](/wiki/Joy_Williams_(singer) "Joy Williams (singer)") & \[John Paul White\](/wiki/John_Paul_White "John Paul White") (songwriters) (2013) "\[Skyfall\](/wiki/Skyfall_(song) "Skyfall (song)")" – \[Adele Atkins\](/wiki/Adele "Adele") & \[Paul Epworth\](/wiki/Paul_Epworth "Paul Epworth") (songwriters) (2014) "\[Let It Go\](/wiki/Let_It_Go "Let It Go")" – \[Kristen Anderson-Lopez\](/wiki/Kristen_Anderson-Lopez "Kristen Anderson-Lopez") & \[Robert Lopez\](/wiki/Robert_Lopez "Robert Lopez") (songwriters) (2015) "\[Glory\](/wiki/Glory_(Common_and_John_Legend_song) "Glory (Common and John Legend song)")" – \[Common\](/wiki/Common_(rapper) "Common (rapper)"), \[Che Smith\](/wiki/Rhymefest "Rhymefest") & \[John Legend\](/wiki/John_Legend "John Legend") (songwriters) (2016) "\[Can't Stop the Feeling\!\](/wiki/Can%27t_Stop_the_Feeling\! "Can't Stop the Feeling\!")" – \[Max Martin\](/wiki/Max_Martin "Max Martin"), \[Shellback\](/wiki/Shellback_(record_producer) "Shellback (record producer)") & \[Justin Timberlake\](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake") (songwriters) (2017) "\[How Far I'll Go\](/wiki/How_Far_I%27ll_Go "How Far I'll Go")" – \[Lin-Manuel Miranda\](/wiki/Lin-Manuel_Miranda "Lin-Manuel Miranda") (songwriter) (2018) "\[Shallow\](/wiki/Shallow_(Lady_Gaga_and_Bradley_Cooper_song) "Shallow (Lady Gaga and Bradley Cooper song)")" – \[Lady Gaga\](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga"), \[Mark Ronson\](/wiki/Mark_Ronson "Mark Ronson"), \[Anthony Rossomando\](/wiki/Anthony_Rossomando "Anthony Rossomando") & \[Andrew Wyatt\](/wiki/Andrew_Wyatt "Andrew Wyatt") (songwriters) (2019) |
| 2020s | "\[I'll Never Love Again\](/wiki/I%27ll_Never_Love_Again "I'll Never Love Again")" – \[Lady Gaga\](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga"), \[Natalie Hemby\](/wiki/Natalie_Hemby "Natalie Hemby"), \[Hillary Lindsey\](/wiki/Hillary_Lindsey "Hillary Lindsey") & Aaron Raitiere (songwriters) (2020) "\[No Time to Die\](/wiki/No_Time_to_Die_(song) "No Time to Die (song)")" – \[Billie Eilish O'Connell\](/wiki/Billie_Eilish "Billie Eilish") & \[Finneas O'Connell\](/wiki/Finneas_O%27Connell "Finneas O'Connell") (songwriters) (2021) "\[All Eyes On Me\](/wiki/All_Eyes_On_Me_(Bo_Burnham_song) "All Eyes On Me (Bo Burnham song)")" – \[Bo Burnham\](/wiki/Bo_Burnham "Bo Burnham") (songwriter) (2022) "\[We Don't Talk About Bruno\](/wiki/We_Don%27t_Talk_About_Bruno "We Don't Talk About Bruno")" – \[Lin-Manuel Miranda\](/wiki/Lin-Manuel_Miranda "Lin-Manuel Miranda") (songwriter) (2023) "\[What Was I Made For?\](/wiki/What_Was_I_Made_For%3F "What Was I Made For?")" – \[Billie Eilish O'Connell\](/wiki/Billie_Eilish "Billie Eilish") & \[Finneas O'Connell\](/wiki/Finneas_O%27Connell "Finneas O'Connell") (songwriters) (2024) "\[It Never Went Away\](/wiki/It_Never_Went_Away "It Never Went Away")" – \[Jon Batiste\](/wiki/Jon_Batiste "Jon Batiste") & \[Dan Wilson\](/wiki/Dan_Wilson_(musician) "Dan Wilson (musician)") (songwriters) (2025) |
